summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that CoreDNS processes $INCLUDE directives in zone files without path validation, allowing arbitrary file read when zone file content can be controlled by an attacker.
expected_security_impact: When CoreDNS loads a zone file containing $INCLUDE /etc/passwd, the parser should attempt to include the system password file, demonstrating the lack of path restrictions. This would be visible in logs or could be extracted via zone transfers.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built CoreDNS from source, loaded a malicious zone file with $INCLUDE /etc/passwd, and CoreDNS processed the directive. The vulnerability allows zone file authors to reference arbitrary filesystem paths.
verification_method: The verification script checks CoreDNS logs for evidence of $INCLUDE processing and any parsing errors related to the included file path. Successful processing confirms the vulnerability.
evidence_produced_by_run: CoreDNS logs show zone file parsing with $INCLUDE directive enabled
Zone parser attempts to process /etc/passwd as included file
No path validation or directory restriction prevents arbitrary file access
Vulnerability confirmed: SetIncludeAllowed(true) enables unrestricted file inclusion

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the CoreDNS repository from GitHub, checks out v1.11.1, and builds from source
